caption: Pemberton Georgetown — Phase 2 living room reveal. Zellige tile hearth, walnut millwork, ivory linen. Photographed at golden hour.
Marble countertops arrived from the Carrara vendor this morning. Wrapped, crated, and headed to the site tomorrow.
A study in warm neutrals: bouclé sofa, aged brass sconces, hand-knotted Moroccan rug. Kalorama project sneak peek.
Site visit day. Client walkthrough of the Georgetown primary suite before the final reveal.
Sourcing Mediterranean tile in the studio today. Every swatch was field-tested against the natural light of the space.
The finished library at Woodley Park — floor-to-ceiling walnut, brass hardware, and a reading nook by the window.
Kitchen renovation reveal: custom cabinetry in warm oak, honed marble island, professional-grade range. Home is the practice.
Layered textures — hand-loomed rug, linen drapery, aged brass. Small dinner party ready.
The powder room I finished last month. Zellige tile floor to ceiling, unlacquered brass fixtures, one perfect mirror.
Design monograph pile on the drafting desk. Currently reading The Geometry of Light by S. Hariri.
Rock Creek Park walk between site visits. Where the design thinking actually happens.
Client presentation day. Boards, swatches, and a lot of coffee. Feeling good about this palette.
The Pemberton kitchen at reveal. Walnut, marble, and morning light. Three years and three projects with this family.
Vendor showroom in Georgetown. Testing three tile options side by side against the client's own natural stone.
Renders are useful. Physical swatches are non-negotiable. This is why we schedule the studio visit before we specify.
Sunday dinner-party table setting. Linen runner, brass candlesticks, wildflowers from the market. The house is a portfolio piece too.
The primary bathroom I renovated in 2024. Featured in a client's own home tour last week. Warm and quiet.
String lights up in the backyard for hosting season. Mature trees, small patio, the good kind of evening.
A finished-room reveal from the Kalorama consult. Backdrop for a small holiday gathering.
Studio thinking day. Three active projects, three client mood boards, one very strong espresso.
A quiet corner for reading — Falls Church study reveal.
Layered lighting plan coming together for the McLean great room.
